A.does: Walks a fiber's pending update queue, applies each update in priority order, and writes the resulting state to the fiber's `memoizedState` and `baseState`.

B.does: Tears down any in-progress work-in-progress tree, creates a fresh `workInProgress` root fiber for the given `FiberRoot` and lanes, and resets all work-loop globals so a new render can begin from scratch.

comparison: prepareFreshStack is called once at the top of the work loop (or to restart it after interruption) to set up the entire render pass, whereas processUpdateQueue is called per-fiber during that render pass to compute each individual fiber's new state from its enqueued updates.
